title: Current System State
organization: Two Marshalls Studios — Studio Operating System (TMS-OS)
summary: Records the current verified operating state and checkpoint of TMS-OS.

sections.title: Purpose
paragraphs: This document records the verified current state of the Two Marshalls Studios Operating System at the historical recovery and documentation reconciliation checkpoint.
Its purpose is to answer one question:
Where does the studio system actually stand right now?
This document is a human-readable reference. Structured studio records remain governed by TMS-OS and StudioDB.

sections.title: Current Studio System
paragraphs: Full Name: Two Marshalls Studios Operating System
Primary Purpose: Serve as the internal command center for games, systems, documentation, development, assets, publishing, marketing, and studio operations.

sections.title: Current Work Session
paragraphs: Session Description: Historical Recovery and Documentation Reconciliation
The current work is focused on recovering, validating, reconciling, and preserving the history and operating knowledge of Two Marshalls Studios.
This work is intended to ensure that the studio does not preserve only its current technical architecture while losing the history of:

sections.title: Current Milestone State
paragraphs: Last Completed Development Milestone: MILE-013 - Fully Modular StudioDB
Current Active Milestone: MILE-014 - Complete Historical Recovery
The development foundation reached a stable checkpoint before the historical recovery work became the active priority.
The current milestone does not replace or redesign the completed StudioDB foundation.
Its purpose is to recover and reconcile the studio knowledge required to make that foundation historically complete and operationally useful.

sections.title: Current Architecture
paragraphs: TMS-OS evolved from a static studio portal into a structured operating system with a shared data architecture.
items: Structured decisions, standards, procedures, and enhancements.

sections.title: Current Source-of-Truth Model
paragraphs: TMS-OS / StudioDB = Structured Source of Truth

sections.title: TMS-OS / StudioDB
paragraphs: Used for structured studio records, relationships, IDs, system state, and machine-readable knowledge.

sections.title: Google Workspace
paragraphs: Used for permanent human-readable documents, procedures, navigation, planning, reference material, and documentation people need to read and use.

sections.title: Chat
paragraphs: Used for active working sessions, analysis, problem solving, drafting, building, and guided execution.
No one layer should silently replace the purpose of another.

sections.title: Studio Operating System
paragraphs: TMS-OS is the current primary studio infrastructure project.
Its role is to become the internal operating environment for managing the studio.

sections.title: Dead Roads
paragraphs: Dead Roads is a third-person survival game project.
Confirmed gameplay work includes gathering resources and an initial crafting quest.

sections.title: 45s
paragraphs: 45s remains a studio game concept but is not the current active development priority.
No change to its status should be inferred without an approved project decision.

sections.title: Documentation State

The Google human-readable documentation layer is now being physically established.

sections.title: Google Documentation State
paragraphs: The permanent top-level Studio Headquarters structure has been created and verified.
START HERE: Preserved but not yet populated
The first controlled population test is STATE-001.

sections.title: Current Operating Rules
paragraphs: The studio continues to operate under these core rules:
items: Do not redesign completed systems without a clear approved reason.
Stay focused on the active work-session goal.
Do not allow enhancements to derail foundation work.
Record enhancement ideas for later review.
Provide complete replacement files when code changes are required.
Do not provide partial code fragments as file replacements.
Commit and push stable completed work.
Document decisions, standards, procedures, bugs, fixes, lessons, technical debt, and enhancements.
Separate verified facts from recommendations and approved decisions.

sections.title: Current Documentation Priority
paragraphs: Complete the human-readable Google documentation layer without creating a competing source of truth.

Verify Google Environment → Create Permanent Headquarters Structure → Define Population Procedure → Test with STATE-001 → Populate and Verify Core Documents → Build START HERE Navigation → Reconcile Google Documentation with TMS-OS → Establish Permanent Documentation Checkpoint

sections.title: Current Risks
paragraphs: The approved control is to populate and verify documents one at a time.
items: treating reconstructed wording as original recovered wording
allowing Google documents to conflict with StudioDB
linking incomplete documents from START HERE
allowing checkpoint numbers to become permanent assumptions
moving too quickly and skipping verification

sections.title: Current Next Action
paragraphs: Then: Populate → Format → Verify → Record Result
No additional core document should be populated until the first procedure test passes.
